reforms.description: Slovenia's National Recovery and Resilience Plan allocates EUR 10 million specifically for research, development, and innovation, aiming to support the green and digital transitions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding is expected to enhance Slovenia's innovation capacity, contributing to economic growth through advancements in green and digital technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan includes specific allocations for R&D and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding represents a significant investment in R&D, which is crucial for supporting Slovenia's transition towards a more innovative and sustainable economy.
reforms.source_quote: Through the NRRP, EUR 532.7 million will be channelled to finance measures supporting the green and digital transitions.

reforms.description: The Bank of Slovenia established an Innovation Hub to facilitate knowledge sharing among financial regulators and companies, aiming to enhance the FinTech sector's growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration and knowledge sharing, the Innovation Hub is expected to stimulate the growth of FinTech startups, enhancing innovation in the financial sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The Bank of Slovenia has established the Innovation Hub.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the Innovation Hub represents a significant step towards enhancing the FinTech ecosystem, although it is not a comprehensive overhaul of the regulatory framework.
reforms.source_quote: The Bank of Slovenia established an Innovation Hub, which is dedicated to sharing knowledge and experiences among financial regulators and companies.

reforms.description: The government introduced a draft law to tax capital gains from cryptocurrency assets, aiming to regulate the cryptocurrency market and increase fiscal gains.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While the tax aims to regulate the cryptocurrency market, its primary focus on fiscal gains may not effectively promote innovation in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: A draft law was adopted by the government in spring 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a capital gains tax on cryptocurrency represents a significant regulatory change, although it does not fully address the broader regulatory framework needed for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: A draft law was adopted by the government in spring 2022 that introduced the taxation of capital gains from cryptocurrency assets.

reforms.description: The government introduced training vouchers for young adults and older people aged 55 and more in 2022 to increase training opportunities and support lifelong learning.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing training vouchers can incentivize skill development among older and younger workers, enhancing their employability and adaptability in the labor market.
reforms.status_evidence: The government introduced training vouchers in 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of training vouchers is a significant step towards enhancing workforce skills, particularly for vulnerable groups, which can have lasting economic benefits.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced training vouchers for young adults and older people aged 55 and more in 2022.

reforms.description: Slovenia's Digital Slovenia 2030 Strategy aims to position the country among the top five most digitalised nations in Europe, focusing on enhancing productivity and addressing the challenges of a declining labor force.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy is expected to foster productivity growth and new business models, which are crucial for economic resilience.
reforms.status_evidence: The new digital transformation strategy, Digital Slovenia 2030, still has to be adopted.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a significant shift in Slovenia's approach to digital transformation, aiming for comprehensive improvements across various sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s ambitious digitalisation strategy (Digital Slovenia 2030 Strategy) aims at putting Slovenia among the five most digitalised countries in Europe.

reforms.description: The establishment of the Government Information Security Office (GISO) aims to enhance national information and cyber security, coordinating efforts across stakeholders and promoting digital security measures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital security is essential for fostering trust in digital technologies, which can enhance overall economic activity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: In 2021, the Government Information Security Office (GISO) was established.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of GISO represents a significant step towards improving national cyber security, which is crucial for the digital economy.
reforms.source_quote: In 2021, the Government Information Security Office (GISO) was established, succeeding the Information Security Administration of the Republic of Slovenia.

reforms.description: Slovenia has implemented a new public tender to co-finance the rollout of open high-capacity broadband networks in underserved areas, aiming to improve connectivity in sparsely populated regions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to enhance broadband access, which is essential for digital transformation and economic growth in underserved areas.
reforms.status_evidence: The public tender to co-finance the rollout was closed in December 2021 and is currently being implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in broadband infrastructure, addressing a critical gap in connectivity that can drive economic development and digital inclusion.
reforms.source_quote: A new public tender to co-finance the rollout of open high-capacity broadband networks (through a programme called 'GOŠO 5') was closed in December 2021 and is currently being implemented.

reforms.description: Slovenia is promoting digitalisation in enterprises, particularly SMEs, through a range of measures including vouchers and consulting services provided by the Digital Innovation Hub. However, these measures are limited in scale and scope, which may restrict their effectiveness in facilitating digital adoption.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance the digital capabilities of SMEs, which can lead to increased productivity and competitiveness in the digital economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government provides a range of measures to promote digitalisation in enterprises.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the measures are not system-oriented and limited in scale, they represent a significant effort to support SMEs in digitalisation, which is crucial for their growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government provides a range of measures to promote digitalisation in enterprises, particularly SMEs that appear well suited to the needs of SMEs.

reforms.description: The new Research and Innovation Activities Act allows public research organisations to become co-owners of spin-offs, aiming to enhance the commercialisation of research outputs. However, the process for establishing these companies is cumbersome, which may hinder the effectiveness of this reform.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating the creation of spin-offs can enhance the transfer of knowledge from public research to the market, potentially leading to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new Research and Innovation Activities Act, which entered into force in January 2022, allows institutions to become co-owners of spin-offs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the policy framework governing the relationship between public research organisations and commercialisation efforts, potentially leading to a more vibrant innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The new Research and Innovation Activities Act, which entered into force in January 2022, allows institutions to become co-owners of spin-offs.

reforms.description: The establishment of a Strategic Council for Entrepreneurship in Education aims to enhance entrepreneurship education across all levels of education, fostering collaboration between researchers and the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the skills and entrepreneurial mindset of students, which can lead to increased start-up creation and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The council was established to formulate an action plan for entrepreneurship education.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards integrating entrepreneurship education into the national education system, which can have lasting impacts on innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: A key development was the 2020 establishment of a Strategic Council for Entrepreneurship in Education to bring together researchers and representatives from the private sector.

reforms.description: Performance-based funding was introduced in 2017 to stimulate collaboration activities in higher education, amounting to 3% of total public funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages universities to engage in collaborative research and innovation activities, potentially leading to increased knowledge transfer and economic benefits.
reforms.status_evidence: Performance-based funding was introduced to stimulate collaboration activities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in funding allocation that incentivizes collaboration, which is crucial for enhancing innovation in higher education.
reforms.source_quote: In 2017, performance-based funding was introduced, amounting to 3% of the total public funding of higher education, to stimulate collaboration activities in higher education.

reforms.description: The creation of technology transfer offices (TTOs) and research centres in many higher education institutions aims to support innovation and knowledge transfer.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates the commercialization of research outputs, enhancing the link between academia and industry, which can drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Many HEIs have created technology transfer offices and research centres to support innovation and knowledge transfer.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of TTOs represents a significant effort to enhance the commercialization of research, which is vital for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In addition, many HEIs have created technology transfer offices (TTOs) and research centres to support innovation and knowledge transfer.

reforms.description: A knowledge and technology transfer consortium was created to associate TTOs from public universities and research centres, offering a pool of technologies and products to private companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances collaboration between public research institutions and private companies, facilitating the transfer of innovative technologies and products.
reforms.status_evidence: A knowledge and technology transfer consortium was created to associate TTOs from public universities and research centres.
reforms.importance_rationale: This consortium represents a significant effort to enhance knowledge transfer and collaboration between academia and industry, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Moreover, such offices and centres represent learning opportunities for researchers to develop cutting edge-technology to respond to the needs of industry.

reforms.description: The Resolution on the National Programme (Master Plan) of Adult Education in the Republic of Slovenia 2021-2030 aims to promote inclusive lifelong learning for adults, including targeted guidance and tailored learning opportunities, particularly in ICT.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing adult education and ICT skills, the reform aims to improve workforce capabilities, thereby supporting economic productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The Resolution was adopted in March 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in adult education policy, aiming to enhance lifelong learning and address skills gaps in the workforce, particularly in ICT.
reforms.source_quote: The Resolution on the National Programme (Master Plan) of Adult Education in the Republic of Slovenia 2021-2030 was adopted in March 2022.

reforms.description: The National Digital Education Action Plan aims to enhance digital infrastructure, e-learning platforms, and teacher training in digital skills, including the introduction of compulsory computer science studies in schools.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital skills among teachers and students is expected to enhance the overall skill level of the workforce, supporting future economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan was developed and announced by the Minister of Education, Science and Sport.
reforms.importance_rationale: The plan represents a significant effort to modernize education and improve digital skills, which are crucial for adapting to a digital economy.
reforms.source_quote: The National Digital Education Action Plan focuses on improving infrastructure, e-learning platforms, teachers’ upskilling and support to schools’ principles in developing digital transformation plans.

reforms.description: A reform of the corporate income tax introduced a special tax relief for companies employing professionals in shortage, aiming to attract skilled workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While the reform aims to attract skilled workers, it risks creating market distortions by subsidizing certain categories of employment over others.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was enacted as part of the corporate income tax changes in 2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant as it introduces a targeted tax relief mechanism to address skill shortages, although it may not fundamentally change the overall tax structure.
reforms.source_quote: In 2021, a reform of the corporate income tax introduced a special tax relief for companies that employ professionals in shortage.

reforms.description: Slovenia plans to introduce innovation vouchers for SMEs to contract R&D support from academia, aiming to enhance collaboration between research institutions and small businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates access to R&D resources for SMEs, promoting innovation and competitiveness in the market.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to introduce innovation vouchers for SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards enhancing SME access to R&D, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Introduce innovation vouchers for SMEs to contract R&D support from academia.

reforms.description: The government aims to provide investors with tax deductibility for start-up and growth financing for innovative start-ups and SMEs, addressing the lack of risk capital in the market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages investment in innovative start-ups, which can lead to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to provide tax deductibility for start-up financing.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly addresses the financing gap for innovative start-ups, which is critical for their growth and sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: Provide investors with tax deductibility for start-up and growth financing for innovative start-ups and SMEs.
